title: Infosys Opens New Zurich Office to Support AI-Led Enterprise Transformation in Switzerland
detailed_summary: Infosys inaugurated a new office in Zurich, Switzerland, relocating its Swiss headquarters to The Circle at Zurich Airport. The office strengthens Infosys' 25-year presence in Switzerland and is positioned as a hub for innovation and co-creation with clients across sectors including manufacturing, financial services, life sciences, energy & services, telecom, and retail. Infosys said the office will use Infosys Topaz and Infosys Cobalt to support Swiss enterprises in digitalization, operational resilience, data-scale use cases, improved experiences, security, and compliance. The document also notes a CSR grant to Little Scientists for STEM education through Infosys Springboard.

key_facts.fact: Infosys inaugurated its new office in Zurich, Switzerland, and relocated its Swiss headquarters to The Circle at Zurich Airport.
key_facts.section_heading: Infosys Expands Its Footprint in Switzerland with a New Zurich Office to Accelerate Enterprise AI Journeys

key_facts.fact: The Zurich office marks a milestone in Infosys' 25-year presence in Switzerland.
key_facts.section_heading: Infosys Expands Its Footprint in Switzerland with a New Zurich Office to Accelerate Enterprise AI Journeys

key_facts.fact: The new location is intended to serve as a hub for innovation and co-creation and bring teams closer to clients across manufacturing, financial services, life sciences, energy & services, telecom, and retail sectors, among others.
key_facts.section_heading: Infosys Expands Its Footprint in Switzerland with a New Zurich Office to Accelerate Enterprise AI Journeys

key_facts.fact: Infosys said the Zurich office will support Swiss enterprises' digitalization journey by leveraging Infosys Topaz and Infosys Cobalt.
key_facts.section_heading: Infosys Expands Its Footprint in Switzerland with a New Zurich Office to Accelerate Enterprise AI Journeys

key_facts.fact: Infosys cited its existing Swiss presence in Geneva, Basel, Baden, Lausanne, and Baar.
key_facts.section_heading: Infosys Expands Its Footprint in Switzerland with a New Zurich Office to Accelerate Enterprise AI Journeys

key_facts.fact: Infosys has hosted over 100 students from leading Swiss institutions through its InStep internship program over the last two decades.
key_facts.section_heading: Infosys Expands Its Footprint in Switzerland with a New Zurich Office to Accelerate Enterprise AI Journeys

esg_or_csr_items.item: Infosys awarded a grant to Little Scientists for a one-year partnership to advance STEM education through a multilingual virtual learning environment on Infosys Springboard, aiming to reach 1,000 students in its first year.
esg_or_csr_items.section_heading: Infosys Expands Its Footprint in Switzerland with a New Zurich Office to Accelerate Enterprise AI Journeys

important_text_spans.text: today inaugurated its new office in Zurich, Switzerland. Relocating the Swiss headquarters to The Circle at Zurich Airport marks a major milestone in Infosys' 25-year presence in the country
important_text_spans.section_heading: Infosys Expands Its Footprint in Switzerland with a New Zurich Office to Accelerate Enterprise AI Journeys
important_text_spans.reason: Supports the main operational expansion and headquarters relocation.

important_text_spans.text: By leveraging Infosys Topaz, an AI-first offering powered by generative AI technologies, and Infosys Cobalt, a set of services, solutions and platforms for enterprises to accelerate their cloud journey, the Zurich office will support Swiss enterprises accelerate their digitalization journey.
important_text_spans.section_heading: Infosys Expands Its Footprint in Switzerland with a New Zurich Office to Accelerate Enterprise AI Journeys
important_text_spans.reason: Supports the strategic AI and cloud focus of the Zurich office.

processing_notes: No financial metrics, contract value, guidance, or new commercial deal terms were disclosed. Infosys Topaz and Infosys Cobalt were treated as supporting capabilities rather than distinct product launch events.
